| MCP:kEIJff | 'use strict'
| MCP:kEIJff | const path = require('path')
| MCP:kEIJff | const defaultSettings = require('./src/settings.js')
| MCP:kEIJff | const AutoImport = require('unplugin-auto-import/webpack')
| MCP:kEIJff | const Components = require('unplugin-vue-components/webpack')
| MCP:kEIJff | const { ElementPlusResolver } = require('unplugin-vue-components/resolvers')
| MCP:kEIJff | function resolve(dir) {
| MCP:kEIJff |   return path.join(__dirname, dir)
| MCP:kEIJff | }
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff | const name = defaultSettings.title || 'vue Admin Template' // page title
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff | // If your port is set to 80,
| MCP:kEIJff | // use administrator privileges to execute the command line.
| MCP:kEIJff | // For example, Mac: sudo npm run
| MCP:kEIJff | // You can change the port by the following methods:
| MCP:kEIJff | // port = 9528 npm run dev OR npm run dev --port = 9528
| MCP:kEIJff | const port = process.env.port || process.env.npm_config_port || 9528 // dev port
| MCP:kEIJff | const isDev = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'
| MCP:kEIJff | // All configuration item explanations can be find in https://cli.vuejs.org/config/
| MCP:kEIJff | module.exports = {
| MCP:kEIJff |   /**
| MCP:kEIJff |    * You will need to set publicPath if you plan to deploy your site under a sub path,
| MCP:kEIJff |    * for example GitHub Pages. If you plan to deploy your site to https://foo.github.io/bar/,
| MCP:kEIJff |    * then publicPath should be set to "/bar/".
| MCP:kEIJff |    * In most cases please use '/' !!!
| MCP:kEIJff |    * Detail: https://cli.vuejs.org/config/#publicpath
| MCP:kEIJff |    */
| MCP:kEIJff |   publicPath: isDev ? '/' : '/vue3-admin-template/',
| MCP:kEIJff |   outputDir: 'dist',
| MCP:kEIJff |   assetsDir: 'static',
| MCP:kEIJff |   lintOnSave: isDev,
| MCP:kEIJff |   productionSourceMap: true,
| MCP:kEIJff |   css: {
| MCP:kEIJff |     sourceMap: isDev,
| MCP:kEIJff |   },
| MCP:kEIJff |   devServer: {
| MCP:kEIJff |     port: port,
| MCP:kEIJff |     open: false,
| MCP:kEIJff |     overlay: {
| MCP:kEIJff |       warnings: false,
| MCP:kEIJff |       errors: true,
| MCP:kEIJff |     },
| MCP:kEIJff |     before: require('./mock/mock-server.js'),
| MCP:kEIJff |   },
| MCP:kEIJff |   configureWebpack: {
| MCP:kEIJff |     // provide the app's title in webpack's name field, so that
| MCP:kEIJff |     // it can be accessed in index.html to inject the correct title.
| MCP:kEIJff |     name: name,
| MCP:kEIJff |     resolve: {
| MCP:kEIJff |       alias: {
| MCP:kEIJff |         '@': resolve('src'),
| MCP:kEIJff |       },
| MCP:kEIJff |     },
| MCP:kEIJff |     plugins: [
| MCP:kEIJff |       AutoImport({
| MCP:kEIJff |         resolvers: [
| MCP:kEIJff |           ElementPlusResolver({
| MCP:kEIJff |             importStyle: false,
| MCP:kEIJff |           }),
| MCP:kEIJff |         ],
| MCP:kEIJff |       }),
| MCP:kEIJff |       Components({
| MCP:kEIJff |         resolvers: [ElementPlusResolver()],
| MCP:kEIJff |       }),
| MCP:kEIJff |     ],
| MCP:kEIJff |   },
| MCP:kEIJff |   chainWebpack(config) {
| MCP:kEIJff |     // it can improve the speed of the first screen, it is recommended to turn on preload
| MCP:kEIJff |     config.plugin('preload').tap(() => [
| MCP:kEIJff |       {
| MCP:kEIJff |         rel: 'preload',
| MCP:kEIJff |         // to ignore runtime.js
| MCP:kEIJff |         // https://github.com/vuejs/vue-cli/blob/dev/packages/@vue/cli-service/lib/config/app.js#L171
| MCP:kEIJff |         fileBlacklist: [/\.map$/, /hot-update\.js$/, /runtime\..*\.js$/],
| MCP:kEIJff |         include: 'initial',
| MCP:kEIJff |       },
| MCP:kEIJff |     ])
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |     // when there are many pages, it will cause too many meaningless requests
| MCP:kEIJff |     config.plugins.delete('prefetch')
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |     // set svg-sprite-loader
| MCP:kEIJff |     config.module.rule('svg').exclude.add(resolve('src/icons')).end()
| MCP:kEIJff |     config.module
| MCP:kEIJff |       .rule('icons')
| MCP:kEIJff |       .test(/\.svg$/)
| MCP:kEIJff |       .include.add(resolve('src/icons'))
| MCP:kEIJff |       .end()
| MCP:kEIJff |       .use('svg-sprite-loader')
| MCP:kEIJff |       .loader('svg-sprite-loader')
| MCP:kEIJff |       .options({
| MCP:kEIJff |         symbolId: 'icon-[name]',
| MCP:kEIJff |       })
| MCP:kEIJff |       .end()
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |     config.when(process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'development', (config) => {
| MCP:kEIJff |       config
| MCP:kEIJff |         .plugin('ScriptExtHtmlWebpackPlugin')
| MCP:kEIJff |         .after('html')
| MCP:kEIJff |         .use('script-ext-html-webpack-plugin', [
| MCP:kEIJff |           {
| MCP:kEIJff |             // `runtime` must same as runtimeChunk name. default is `runtime`
| MCP:kEIJff |             inline: /runtime\..*\.js$/,
| MCP:kEIJff |           },
| MCP:kEIJff |         ])
| MCP:kEIJff |         .end()
| MCP:kEIJff |       config.optimization.splitChunks({
| MCP:kEIJff |         chunks: 'all',
| MCP:kEIJff |         cacheGroups: {
| MCP:kEIJff |           libs: {
| MCP:kEIJff |             name: 'chunk-libs',
| MCP:kEIJff |             test: /[\\/]node_modules[\\/]/,
| MCP:kEIJff |             priority: 10,
| MCP:kEIJff |             chunks: 'initial', // only package third parties that are initially dependent
| MCP:kEIJff |           },
| MCP:kEIJff |           elementUI: {
| MCP:kEIJff |             name: 'chunk-elementPlus', // split elementUI into a single package
| MCP:kEIJff |             priority: 20, // the weight needs to be larger than libs and app or it will be packaged into libs or app
| MCP:kEIJff |             test: /[\\/]node_modules[\\/]_?element-plus(.*)|[\\/]node_modules[\\/]_?@element-plus(.*)/, // in order to adapt to cnpm
| MCP:kEIJff |           },
| MCP:kEIJff |           commons: {
| MCP:kEIJff |             name: 'chunk-commons',
| MCP:kEIJff |             test: resolve('src/components'), // can customize your rules
| MCP:kEIJff |             minChunks: 3, //  minimum common number
| MCP:kEIJff |             priority: 5,
| MCP:kEIJff |             reuseExistingChunk: true,
| MCP:kEIJff |           },
| MCP:kEIJff |         },
| MCP:kEIJff |       })
| MCP:kEIJff |       // https:// webpack.js.org/configuration/optimization/#optimizationruntimechunk
| MCP:kEIJff |       config.optimization.runtimeChunk('single')
| MCP:kEIJff |     })
| MCP:kEIJff |   },
| MCP:kEIJff | }

| MCP:kEIJff | import axios from 'axios'
| MCP:kEIJff | import { ElMessageBox, ElMessage } from 'element-plus'
| MCP:kEIJff | import store from '@/store'
| MCP:kEIJff | import { getToken } from '@/utils/auth'
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff | // create an axios instance
| MCP:kEIJff | const service = axios.create({
| MCP:kEIJff |   baseURL: process.env.VUE_APP_BASE_API, // url = base url + request url
| MCP:kEIJff |   // withCredentials: true, // send cookies when cross-domain requests
| MCP:kEIJff |   timeout: 5000, // request timeout
| MCP:kEIJff | })
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff | // request interceptor
| MCP:kEIJff | service.interceptors.request.use(
| MCP:kEIJff |   (config) => {
| MCP:kEIJff |     // do something before request is sent
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |     if (store.getters.token) {
| MCP:kEIJff |       // let each request carry token
| MCP:kEIJff |       // ['X-Token'] is a custom headers key
| MCP:kEIJff |       // please modify it according to the actual situation
| MCP:kEIJff |       config.headers['X-Token'] = getToken()
| MCP:kEIJff |     }
| MCP:kEIJff |     return config
| MCP:kEIJff |   },
| MCP:kEIJff |   (error) => {
| MCP:kEIJff |     // do something with request error
| MCP:kEIJff |     console.log(error) // for debug
| MCP:kEIJff |     return Promise.reject(error)
| MCP:kEIJff |   }
| MCP:kEIJff | )
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff | // response interceptor
| MCP:kEIJff | service.interceptors.response.use(
| MCP:kEIJff |   /**
| MCP:kEIJff |    * If you want to get http information such as headers or status
| MCP:kEIJff |    * Please return  response => response
| MCP:kEIJff |    */
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |   /**
| MCP:kEIJff |    * Determine the request status by custom code
| MCP:kEIJff |    * Here is just an example
| MCP:kEIJff |    * You can also judge the status by HTTP Status Code
| MCP:kEIJff |    */
| MCP:kEIJff |   (response) => {
| MCP:kEIJff |     const res = response.data
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |     // if the custom code is not 20000, it is judged as an error.
| MCP:kEIJff |     if (res.code !== 20000) {
| MCP:kEIJff |       ElMessage({
| MCP:kEIJff |         message: res.message || 'Error',
| MCP:kEIJff |         type: 'error',
| MCP:kEIJff |         duration: 5 * 1000,
| MCP:kEIJff |       })
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |       // 50008: Illegal token; 50012: Other clients logged in; 50014: Token expired;
| MCP:kEIJff |       if (res.code === 50008 || res.code === 50012 || res.code === 50014) {
| MCP:kEIJff |         // to re-login
| MCP:kEIJff |         ElMessageBox.confirm(
| MCP:kEIJff |           '您已登出，可以取消停留在此页面上，或者再次登录',
| MCP:kEIJff |           '重新登录',
| MCP:kEIJff |           {
| MCP:kEIJff |             confirmButtonText: '重新登录',
| MCP:kEIJff |             cancelButtonText: '离开',
| MCP:kEIJff |             type: 'warning',
| MCP:kEIJff |           }
| MCP:kEIJff |         ).then(() => {
| MCP:kEIJff |           store.dispatch('user/resetToken').then(() => {
| MCP:kEIJff |             location.reload()
| MCP:kEIJff |           })
| MCP:kEIJff |         })
| MCP:kEIJff |       }
| MCP:kEIJff |       return Promise.reject(new Error(res.message || 'Error'))
| MCP:kEIJff |     } else {
| MCP:kEIJff |       return res
| MCP:kEIJff |     }
| MCP:kEIJff |   },
| MCP:kEIJff |   (error) => {
| MCP:kEIJff |     console.log('err' + error) // for debug
| MCP:kEIJff |     ElMessage({
| MCP:kEIJff |       message: error.message,
| MCP:kEIJff |       type: 'error',
| MCP:kEIJff |       duration: 5 * 1000,
| MCP:kEIJff |     })
| MCP:kEIJff |     return Promise.reject(error)
| MCP:kEIJff |   }
| MCP:kEIJff | )
| MCP:kEIJff |

| MCP:kEIJff | const chokidar = require('chokidar')
| MCP:kEIJff | const bodyParser = require('body-parser')
| MCP:kEIJff | const chalk = require('chalk')
| MCP:kEIJff | const path = require('path')
| MCP:kEIJff | const Mock = require('mockjs')
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff | const mockDir = path.join(process.cwd(), 'mock')
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff | function registerRoutes(app) {
| MCP:kEIJff |   let mockLastIndex
| MCP:kEIJff |   const { mocks } = require('./index.js')
| MCP:kEIJff |   const mocksForServer = mocks.map((route) => {
| MCP:kEIJff |     return responseFake(route.url, route.type, route.response)
| MCP:kEIJff |   })
| MCP:kEIJff |   for (const mock of mocksForServer) {
| MCP:kEIJff |     app[mock.type](mock.url, mock.response)
| MCP:kEIJff |     mockLastIndex = app._router.stack.length
| MCP:kEIJff |   }
| MCP:kEIJff |   const mockRoutesLength = Object.keys(mocksForServer).length
| MCP:kEIJff |   return {
| MCP:kEIJff |     mockRoutesLength: mockRoutesLength,
| MCP:kEIJff |     mockStartIndex: mockLastIndex - mockRoutesLength,
| MCP:kEIJff |   }
| MCP:kEIJff | }
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff | function unregisterRoutes() {
| MCP:kEIJff |   Object.keys(require.cache).forEach((i) => {
| MCP:kEIJff |     if (i.includes(mockDir)) {
| MCP:kEIJff |       delete require.cache[require.resolve(i)]
| MCP:kEIJff |     }
| MCP:kEIJff |   })
| MCP:kEIJff | }
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff | // for mock server
| MCP:kEIJff | const responseFake = (url, type, respond) => {
| MCP:kEIJff |   return {
| MCP:kEIJff |     url: new RegExp(`${process.env.VUE_APP_BASE_API}${url}`),
| MCP:kEIJff |     type: type || 'get',
| MCP:kEIJff |     response(req, res) {
| MCP:kEIJff |       console.log('request invoke:' + req.path)
| MCP:kEIJff |       res.json(
| MCP:kEIJff |         Mock.mock(respond instanceof Function ? respond(req, res) : respond)
| MCP:kEIJff |       )
| MCP:kEIJff |     },
| MCP:kEIJff |   }
| MCP:kEIJff | }
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff | module.exports = (app) => {
| MCP:kEIJff |   // parse app.body
| MCP:kEIJff |   // https://expressjs.com/en/4x/api.html#req.body
| MCP:kEIJff |   app.use(bodyParser.json())
| MCP:kEIJff |   app.use(
| MCP:kEIJff |     bodyParser.urlencoded({
| MCP:kEIJff |       extended: true,
| MCP:kEIJff |     })
| MCP:kEIJff |   )
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |   const mockRoutes = registerRoutes(app)
| MCP:kEIJff |   let mockRoutesLength = mockRoutes.mockRoutesLength
| MCP:kEIJff |   let mockStartIndex = mockRoutes.mockStartIndex
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |   // watch files, hot reload mock server
| MCP:kEIJff |   chokidar
| MCP:kEIJff |     .watch(mockDir, {
| MCP:kEIJff |       ignored: /mock-server/,
| MCP:kEIJff |       ignoreInitial: true,
| MCP:kEIJff |     })
| MCP:kEIJff |     .on('all', (event, path) => {
| MCP:kEIJff |       if (event === 'change' || event === 'add') {
| MCP:kEIJff |         try {
| MCP:kEIJff |           // remove mock routes stack
| MCP:kEIJff |           app._router.stack.splice(mockStartIndex, mockRoutesLength)
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |           // clear routes cache
| MCP:kEIJff |           unregisterRoutes()
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |           const mockRoutes = registerRoutes(app)
| MCP:kEIJff |           mockRoutesLength = mockRoutes.mockRoutesLength
| MCP:kEIJff |           mockStartIndex = mockRoutes.mockStartIndex
| MCP:kEIJff | 
| MCP:kEIJff |           console.log(
| MCP:kEIJff |             chalk.magentaBright(
| MCP:kEIJff |               `\n > Mock Server hot reload success! changed  ${path}`
| MCP:kEIJff |             )
| MCP:kEIJff |           )
| MCP:kEIJff |         } catch (error) {
| MCP:kEIJff |           console.log(chalk.redBright(error))
| MCP:kEIJff |         }
| MCP:kEIJff |       }
| MCP:kEIJff |     })
| MCP:kEIJff | }

data.content:
## How the Template Switches Between Development and Production API Base Paths

### Environment-Based Configuration

The template uses Vue CLI's environment file system (`.env.*` files) to define the API base path for each environment:

| File | `ENV` flag | `VUE_APP_BASE_API` value |
|---|---|---|
| `.env.development` | `development` | `/dev-api` |
| `.env.production` | `production` | `/prod-api` |
| `.env.staging` | `staging` | `/stage-api` |

### How the Switch Works

1. **Build scripts** in `package.json` select the environment:
   - `npm run dev` → Vue CLI serves with the development environment → loads `.env.development` → sets `VUE_APP_BASE_API = '/dev-api'`
   - `npm run build:prod` → Vue CLI builds with the production environment → loads `.env.production` → sets `VUE_APP_BASE_API = '/prod-api'`
   - `npm run build:stage` → Vue CLI builds with the staging environment (via `--mode staging`) → loads `.env.staging` → sets `VUE_APP_BASE_API = '/stage-api'`

2. **Axios request instance** (`src/utils/request.js`) uses the environment variable as its `baseURL`:
   ```js
   const service = axios.create({
     baseURL: process.env.VUE_APP_BASE_API, // url = base url + request url
     ...
   })
   ```

3. **Mock server** (`mock/mock-server.js`) prepends the same `VUE_APP_BASE_API` prefix to mock routes, so during development the mock endpoints are served under `/dev-api/vue-admin-template/...`.

### Mock Login Usernames and Tokens

From `mock/user.js`, the mock login endpoint (`/vue-admin-template/user/login`) accepts these usernames:

| Username | Token returned |
|---|---|
| `admin` | `admin-token` |
| `editor` | `editor-token` |

Any other username returns an error (`code: 60204`, message: "Account and password are incorrect.").

The tokens map to user profiles:
- **`admin-token`**: Super Admin, roles: `['admin']`
- **`editor-token`**: Normal Editor, roles: `['editor']`
